versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "In the past God spoke to our ancestors through the prophets at many times and in various ways, but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom also he made the universe."

context_lines: Hebrews 1:1-2 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of Hebrews.
common_interpretation_lines: The many ways God spoke — including through the *Malak Yahweh* — are now complete. In these last days He has spoken through His Son, who has come in flesh, died, risen, and ascended. The temporary appearances of the pre-incarnate Son gave way to the permanent reality of the Incarnate Son.

deeper_study.title: Who is The Angel of the Lord?
